underlying: RealClearPolitics Trump approval rating average at 11:00 AM ET on May 1, 2026
current_value_source: RealClearPolitics Trump Job Approval, 40.7% approve / 57.4% disapprove (fetched 2026-04-28)
method: Anchor on RCP (the resolution source) at 40.7% on 2026-04-28. Project 3 days forward to May 1 using a slight downward drift (-0.05pp/day) given (a) Reuters/Ipsos 33% poll dropping into the basket on 4/28 will pull RCP, (b) ongoing Iran war headlines and high gas prices, (c) Silver Bulletin and FPO already running 1.7-3.7pp lower so RCP catching down is likely. Sigma 0.45pp captures 3-day RCP polling-average noise: empirical RCP daily moves around 0.10-0.15pp std, so sqrt(3) * 0.13 ~= 0.22 from drift noise + ~0.35 from poll-add discreteness (1-2 polls drop in/out). Treat resolution as continuous Normal(40.55, 0.45) and integrate over each bucket. Probabilities: <39.9 = Phi((39.9-40.55)/0.45) = 7.4%; 39.9-40.1 = Phi(-1.0)-Phi(-1.44) = 8.4%; 40.2-40.4 = Phi(-0.33)-Phi(-0.78) = 15.4%; 40.5-40.7 (mode) = Phi(0.33)-Phi(-0.11) = 17.4%; 40.8-41.0 = Phi(1.0)-Phi(0.56) = 13.5%; 41.1-41.3 = Phi(1.67)-Phi(1.22) = 6.4%; 41.4-41.6 = Phi(2.33)-Phi(1.89) = 1.9%; >41.6 = 1-Phi(2.33) = 1.0%. Sum to within rounding of 1.0 (residual goes to <39.9 left tail since RCP can plausibly drop further on AP-NORC 33 + Reuters 33 entering average).
notes: RCP-resolved (per market rules), so I anchor on RCP not Silver Bulletin/FPO. RCP runs ~1.5-3.5pp higher than other aggregators because of pollster mix (more Rasmussen/InsiderAdvantage). Current RCP 40.7 sits within the modal 40.5-40.7 bucket. Risks: (1) Reuters/Ipsos 33% (4/24-27) and AP-NORC 33% will pull RCP DOWN over next 3 days as they enter the rolling average — this is why I bumped the <40.0 buckets above raw Normal calc and tilted modes left. (2) RCP could ALSO age out an old low-approval poll which would push back up. (3) The 11am ET cutoff captures AM Friday — limited new-poll risk on resolution day itself. Market quotes show heavy mass on 40.5-40.7 (last 0.34) and 40.2-40.4 (last 0.22) — close to my model. I see modest edge: T39.9 bid 0.06 vs my 0.13 (BUY YES), T40.9 bid 0.23 vs my 0.18 (small SELL YES), T41.5 bid 0.04 vs my 0.06 (slight BUY YES). Conf=medium because RCP is well-tracked but 3-day forward drift in volatile news cycle adds genuine sigma.
